assertions.description: When before-and-after is requested, both a pre-change and post-change screenshot are present
assertions.evidence: Two files exist: outputs/before-deployment-frequency.png and outputs/after-deployment-frequency.png.

assertions.description: The screenshot file names clearly identify which dashboard they belong to and whether they are before or after
assertions.evidence: Files are named 'before-deployment-frequency.png' and 'after-deployment-frequency.png' which clearly identify the dashboard and the before/after state.

assertions.description: Exactly two screenshots per affected dashboard (one before, one after)
assertions.evidence: Exactly two files in outputs/: before-deployment-frequency.png and after-deployment-frequency.png.

assertions.description: Screenshots are taken of Grafana at http://localhost:3004 with panels fully loaded (not blank/loading)
assertions.evidence: Both screenshots show the Deployment Frequency dashboard with stat panels loaded (Deployment Frequency: 4.8 deploys/wk, Deployment Success Rate: 86.4%) and charts rendered. However, both before/after screenshots are identical because no actual dashboard JSON change was applied and Grafana was not restarted between screenshots. This is a limitation of the without-skill baseline approach.

notes: The before and after screenshots are identical (same sha256 hash) because without a skill to guide the workflow, the agent did not apply an actual JSON change and restart Grafana between shots. Both screenshots show loaded panels, satisfying the visual quality assertion.
